zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock於1252年出生在英國賀福爾卡芙特 安布時，他的父親Richard of Cornwall43歲，母親Joan de Valletort28歲。他於大約1284年與Joan FitzAlan在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1296年3月30日在英國諾圖波藍德享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾海利斯海利斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock was born in 1252, in Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, England, his father, Richard of Cornwall, was 43 and his mother, Joan de Valletort, was 28. He married Joan FitzAlan about 1284, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 30 March 1296, in Northumberland, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Joan FitzAlan environ 1284, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 30 mars 1296, à Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnockが1252年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクロフト アンブリーで生まれた時，父Richard of Cornwallは43歳，母Joan de Valletortは28歳であった。約1284年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでJoan FitzAlanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1296年3月30日にイギリスノースウンバーランドで44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヘイレスヘイレス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, E: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 하일레스 하일레스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock(이)가 1252년 영국 허퍼드실르 크로프트 아브리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard of Cornwall(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Valletort(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1284년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Joan FitzAlan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년 3월 30일 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 하일레스 하일레스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock nasceu em 1252, em Croft Ambrey, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard of Cornwall, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Valletort, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan FitzAlan aproximadamente 1284, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 30 de março de 1296, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Hailes Abbey, Hailes, Gloucestershire, E: хеилесь эбби, хеилесь, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock родился в 1252 г. в местности крофт эмбри, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard of Cornwall было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Valletort, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan FitzAlan около 1284 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 30 марта 1296 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности хеилесь эбби, хеилесь, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
